  browser stack-fallback path (using vi.resetModules() to force a fresh
  module load without AsyncLocalStorage)
- Add missing tests in tests/chronos.test.js: createChronos throw,
  context-edge creation via contextId, and subgraph() query

fix(payroll): role-aware compound key allows idea-originator + implementer payouts to same wallet (#196)

* Initial plan

* fix: payroll queue blocking error — remove duplicate on-chain keys, fix fractional amounts

Root cause: issues #187, #189, #192 each had @TheJollyLaMa listed as both
'implementer' AND 'idea-originator' with the same wallet address. Both entries
produced identical entryKey() compound keys, causing the second entry to revert
with 'Treasury: issue already paid' during batchPayContributors.

Changes:
- payroll-queue.json: remove 3 duplicate TheJollyLaMa 'implementer' entries
  (#187, #189, #192); idea-originator entries are kept intact
- contributor-accounts.json: decrease TheJollyLaMa bnutPending by 2.4 BNUT
  (3 × 0.8 removed) → 43.2
- js/app.js: replace parseInt → parseFloat (with positive-number guard) in both
  per-row and batch payout paths so fractional bounties (0.8, 0.4 BNUT) are
  sent at the correct value instead of being floored to 0 → rounded up to 1
- scripts/validatePayrollQueue.js: add check 5b — detects duplicate on-chain
  compound keys (issueRef:contributorAddress) that would cause a contract revert

Co-authored-by: TheJollyLaMa <75486638+TheJollyLaMa@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/TheJollyLaMa/BigNuten_Vanilla/sessions/258e1d9c-77b3-4f6d-ba27-3dbab48f9566

* fix(payroll): role-aware compound key — idea-originator + implementer coexist for same wallet

When a contributor both originates an idea (20% credit) and implements the
solution (80%), two independent queue entries exist for the same wallet+issue.
The old entryKey() used `issueRef:wallet` (no role), so both entries produced
an identical on-chain key — the contract reverted on the second with
"Treasury: issue already paid".

Fix: include role in compound key when present:
  issueRef:wallet:implementer   — 80% implementation share
  issueRef:wallet:idea-originator — 20% idea-credit share
  issueRef:wallet               — legacy entries (no role, unchanged)

Changes:
- js/app.js entryKey(): append :role suffix when p.role is set
- js/app.js per-row Send button: fix p.issueRef → entryKey(p) for the
  contract call (was using raw ref instead of compound key)
- js/treasury.js getContributorPaidEvents: update strip regex to also
  remove :role suffix for display
- payroll-queue.json: restore 3 TheJollyLaMa implementer entries for
  #187/#189/#192 (wrongly removed in previous PR — they are legitimate pay)
- contributor-accounts.json: restore TheJollyLaMa bnutPending 43.2→45.6
- scripts/validatePayrollQueue.js onChainKey(): include role in key
- bounty-bot.yml (Job1 + Job2): role-aware implementer deduplication so
  a contributor who is also idea-originator is not skipped as implementer

Fix the PoP flow in the console app (#887)

* Fix PoP flow in the test app

Currently the test app sends both PoP parameters (see placeholder_auth_scheme definition)
and also passes req_cnf and token type. There parameters are not
compatible. If application passes PoP parameters, then MSAL (or the
broker) owns the key and does the signing of the SHR. If application
passes req_cnf, then application owns the key and creates and signs the
SHR, so in this case it should not pass PoP parameters.

The main flow is the first one - application passes only PoP parameters
and either MSAL or the broker owns the key and generates the SHR.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

commit.message:
ci: fix safe.directory for container-based test jobs

actions/checkout@v4 sets safe.directory using a temporary HOME
override that doesn't persist for subsequent steps. When
vcs-versioning runs git during `pip install -e .`, it uses the
real HOME (/root) where the config doesn't exist, causing
"dubious ownership" failures.

Add explicit `git config --global --add safe.directory` before
pip install in all 6 container-based jobs.
